rulings.Case Title: MARLENE RAMOS ET AL VS. JAY POINDEXTER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 21, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT EDUART HOXHA MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to strike is granted as to the Plaintiff Marlene Ramos. Any claims by Marlene against the moving defendant, that arouse during Marlene's tenancy and moving defendant's management of such tenancy should have been brought as a compulsory cross-complaint in the prior action. The motion to strike as to Jose's claims is DENIED. Jose was not a party to the prior action. Motion to strike the request for punitive damages is DENIED. Claims against the moving defendant sound in tort/based on statute, and do not arise out of a contract =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC ET AL VS. ELENA ASTURIAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Complaint Anti- Slapp Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 21, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT ELENA ASTURIAS AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT ANTI- SLAPP PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 425.16 is DENIED. The Court notes that the original Anti-SLAPP was filed (July 7, 2023) more than 60 days after personal service of the Summons and Complaint (April 24, 2023). Additional time passed between the lifting of the bankruptcy stay and the filing of the operative Amended Notice. The Court has discretion to deny the motion on that basis alone. CCP 425.16(f). The Court further notes that no courtesy copies of the Amended Notice or prior filings have been delivered to the Court in violation of SFRL 2.7B and Court's November 7, 2023 minute order. (Copies of opposition papers received). This failure is an independent reason for the Court to deny the motion in its discretion. Given the amount of work performed by the Plaintiff (see opposition) the Court will rule on the merits and DENY the motion. Under the standard articulated in Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376, 384-385 ("We have described this second step as a "summary-judgment-like procedure." The court does not weigh evidence or resolve conflicting factual claims. Its inquiry is limited to whether the plaintiff has stated a legally sufficient claim and made prima facie factual showing sufficient to sustain a favorable judgment. It accepts the plaintiff's evidence as true, and evaluates the defendant's showing only to determine if it defeats the plaintiff's claim as a matter of law. " [C]laims with the requisite minimal merit may proceed." ), the Court finds that the Plaintiff met its burden under the second prong of CCP 425.16 to show probability of prevailing on the merits on its cause of action for malicious prosecution: that the Underlying Lawsuit (1) was commenced by or at the direction of the defendant and was pursued to a legal termination in plaintiffs', favor; (2) was brought [or continued] without probable cause ; and (3) was initiated with malice. Bertero v. National General Corp., (1974) 13 Cal.3d 43. As evidence submitted by the Defendant may only be evaluated "to determine if it defeats the plaintiff's claims as a matter or law," the Court reviewed all evidence/ declarations submitted by the defendants and finds that none of them defeat the claims as a matter of law, instead they create factual dispute that cannot be resolved on an Anti-Slapp motion under Baral =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
